name: mcp-broker
description: Run the MCP Broker as a local server and talk to its built-in introspection tools.
long_description: Packages @cyanmycelium/mcp-broker as an installable MCP bundle. On launch the broker starts and the host is bridged over stdio to the reserved `_all` aggregate slot, which unions the tools and prompts of every opted-in provider into a single MCP server. The broker also keeps listening on its configured port, so other MCP servers can tunnel in over WebSocket and become reachable to clients on the Streamable HTTP, SSE and raw WebSocket transports. The stdio host therefore sees the live aggregate of all connected providers.

tools.name: _broker-broker_info
tools.description: Report the broker's identity, version and configuration.

tools.name: _broker-providers_list
tools.description: List the provider slots currently registered with the broker.

tools.name: _broker-provider_status
tools.description: Report the connection status of a given provider slot.

port.title: Broker port
port.description: TCP port the broker listens on for provider tunnels and client transports.

host.title: Bind address
host.description: Network interface to bind. 127.0.0.1 keeps the broker private to this machine; 0.0.0.0 exposes it on the local network.

locale.title: Locale
locale.description: Locale for the broker's tool descriptions (ISO 639-1 base, e.g. en, fr).
